title: FST4W
short description: FST4W is an amateur radio digital protocol designed particularly for the LF and MF bands, for quasi-beacon transmissions of WSPR-style messages. FST4W uses 4-GFSK modulation and offers T/R sequence lengths of 120, 300, 900, and 1800 seconds.
description:
**FST4W** is an amateur radio digital protocol designed particularly for the [LF](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/LF) and [MF](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/MF) bands, for quasi-beacon transmissions of [WSPR](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/WSPR)-style messages. FST4W uses 4-GFSK modulation and offers transmit/receive sequence lengths of 120, 300, 900, and 1800 seconds. FST4W is derived from [FST4](https://www.sigidwiki.com/wiki/FST4) which is used for two-way contacts (QSOs).

FST4W do not require the strict, independent time synchronization and phase locking of modes like EbNaut. FST4W-120 SNR threshold is about 1.8 dB lower than WSPR.

{|class=wikitable style="text-align: center"
|+ Basic parameters of all FST4W submodes
|-
! T/R period (s) !! Symbol length (s)!! Tone spacing (Hz) !! Bandwidth (Hz) !! Minimum SNR for decoding (dB)
|-
| 120 || 0.68 || 1.46 || 5.9 || -32.8
|-
| 300 || 1.79 || 0.56 || 2.2 || -36.8
|-
| 900 || 5.56 || 0.18 || 0.72 || -41.7
|-
| 1800 || 11.2 || 0.089 || 0.36 || -44.8
|}
